name: email_change_notify

metadata.description: Security notification sent to the OLD email address when a user requests an email change (sent by on_email_change_request via user.send_template_email).
notes: `user` is a dict (user.to_dict('basic')) representing the current account holder.
`new_email` is the new email address the user has requested to change to.
This email is sent to the current (old) address as a security notice — no confirmation token is included.
The user can cancel the pending change by logging in and calling POST /api/auth/email/change/cancel.
If the user did not initiate this request, they should cancel immediately or contact support.

subject_template: Your email address is being changed
text_template:
Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

This is a security notice. A request was made to change the email address on your account to:
{{ new_email }}

If you made this request, no action is needed. You will receive a separate confirmation email at the new address.

If you did NOT make this request, cancel it immediately by logging in and visiting your account security settings, or contact support right away.

The change will only take effect once confirmed via the link sent to the new address. Until then, your current email address remains active.
html_template:
Your email address is being changed

Hello{% if user.display_name %} {{ user.display_name }}{% endif %},

This is a security notice. A request was made to change the email address on your account to {{ new_email }}.

Didn't request this? Cancel the change immediately by logging in and visiting your account security settings, or contact support right away.

If you made this request, no action is needed here. A separate confirmation email has been sent to the new address. Your current email address remains active until the change is confirmed.

Sent by Django-MOJO
